data.title: Regions Financial Non-GAAP EPS of $0.57 beats by $0.04, revenue of $1.87B beats by $60M
data.description:
• Regions Financial press release (NYSE:RF): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.57 beats by $0.04.
  • Revenue of $1.87B (+0.5% Y/Y) beats by $60M.

data.title: MGP Ingredients stock slides after weak Q3 numbers weigh on full-year outlook
data.description:
MGP Ingredients (NASDAQ:MGPI) has lowered its full-year outlook, citing industry headwinds that are likely to persist at least through the rest of the year.

The spirits producer now expects fiscal 2024 sales of $695M to $705M (vs. prior guidance of $742M to $756M) and adjusted basic earnings per share of $5.55 to $5.65, below prior outlook of $6.12 to $6.23.

“We are disappointed with our third quarter results and fourth quarter outlook. Soft alcohol spirits category trends and elevated industry-wide whiskey inventories are putting greater than expected pressure on our brown goods business with a larger impact on our smaller, craft customer base,” said David Bratcher, CEO and president of MGP Ingredients.

The company expects a 24% Y/Y decline in Q3 sales ($161.5M). Excluding the impact of the Atchison distillery, sales are expected to decline 14%. Q3 adjusted net income is also expected to decline 5%.

MGP Ingredients (MGPI) will report results for the third quarter on October 31, 2024. Shares were down around 20% premarket on Friday.

data.title: Stock index futures higher boosted by Netflix's earnings beat
data.description:
Stock index futures rose on Friday, as Netflix's (NFLX) third quarter earnings beat helped bring cheer to investors.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) +0.2%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) +0.5% and Dow futures (INDU) were unchanged.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was unchanged at 4.10%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) was flat at 3.98%.

Streaming giant Netflix (NFLX) rose 5% in premarket trade after it beat both its top and bottom line estimates in the third quarter. The firm also topped expectations for user growth.

Wall Street on Thursday scaled a fresh all-time intraday high on a boost from technology stocks and a robust retail sales report that bolstered expectations of a soft landing, but then gave up those gains in the afternoon and eventually ended largely flat.

"The main catalyst for the day's market moves was another batch of upbeat U.S. data, which dampened any immediate fears about a potential recession," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said.

U.S. data once again reminded investors of the wisdom of never underestimating the hedonism of the US consumer. September retail sales were strong, UBS' Paul Donovan said.

"ECB yesterday for the first time this cycle. Meanwhile, markets priced out the likelihood of the Federal Reserve doing the same at every meeting over the next few months," Reid added.

The European Central Bank cut its policy rates by 25 basis points, as expected, on Thursday, its third cut of the year.

On the economic side, the housing starts and permits report will land before the bell.

There are also a few Fed speakers, including Fed Atlanta President Raphael Bostic and Fed Minneapolis President Neel Kashkari.

data.title: Biggest stock movers Friday: NFLX, ISRG, and more
data.description:
Stock futures edged higher Friday morning, positioning the Dow and S&P 500 to mark their longest weekly winning streaks of 2024.

Here are some of Friday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • Netflix (NFLX) shares soared 5% after a strong Q3, with 15% topline growth and an operating income margin rate of 29.6%. Global streaming paid memberships grew 14.4% year-over-year, while free cash flow rose 16% to $2.2B, prompting Netflix to raise its full-year FCF guidance to $6.0B-$6.5B. For Q4, the streaming giant projects 15% revenue growth, with higher paid net additions and a 22% operating margin, a 5-point improvement from last year. Netflix forecasts 2024 revenue growth at the high end of 14%-15%, with an operating margin of 27%, and expects FY2025 revenue of $43B-$44B and a 28% margin.
  • Intuitive Surgical (ISRG) shares rose over 6% following Q3 results that exceeded expectations. The company, known for its Da Vinci surgical systems, placed 379 machines, up from 312 in the same period last year. Net income grew ~28% Y/Y, with EPS increasing to $1.56 from $1.16. Intuitive Surgical reported no significant impact from COVID-19 on procedure volumes in the first nine months of 2024 and ended the quarter with ~$8.3B in cash, up from ~$7.3B at the end of 2023.
  • Chinese stocks surged on Friday, marking their first gain in four sessions, as the People’s Bank of China initiated previously announced measures to bolster the market. The central bank introduced two programs that will inject up to 800B yuan ($113B) into the stock market. So far, 20 securities and fund firms have been authorized to access liquidity from the central bank to purchase equities. Key stocks leading the rally included Bilibili (BILI) up 8%, NIO (NIO) up 5.6%, JD.com (JD) up 5%, Li Auto (LI) up 6.5%, XPeng (XPEV) up 7%, NetEase (NTES) up 5%, and Baidu (BIDU) up 4%, among many more.

data.title: Schlumberger to sell Palliser Block interests
data.description:
Schlumberger (NYSE:SLB) has agreed to sell its interests in the Palliser Block located in Alberta, Canada.

Financial terms of the deal were not disclosed. The transaction, which is subject to regulatory approval and other customary closing conditions, is expected to close late in the fourth quarter of 2024.

The Palliser Block consists of oil and gas wells, surface facilities, a pipeline network and certain oil and gas development rights. SLB and Torxen Energy had purchased the Palliser crude oil and natural gas assets from Cenovus Energy in 2017.

The sale announcement comes ahead of SLB’s third quarter results due today. Analysts expect the energy technology company to earn $0.88 (+12.8% Y/Y) on revenue of $9.3B (+11.9% Y/Y).

data.title: Maxim Power repays senior credit facility
data.description:
• Maxim Power (TSX:MXG:CA) announced repayment of amounts owing within the senior credit facility.

  • The corporation has voluntarily repaid the outstanding principal on both the fixed rate construction facility and the bank term facility 1, within the senior credit facility, for a total principal repayment of $49.9 million.

data.title: European indexes mixed after ECB cuts rates
data.description:
London (UKX) -0.27% UK's retail sales rise more than expected in September.

Germany (DAX:IND) +0.06% after closing at a fresh record level the day before.

France (CAC:IND) +0.24%

Slovakia posted a current account deficit of €251.3 million in August.

The unemployment rate in Sweden climbed to 8.2% in September.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) moved 0.11% higher, as traders digest lackluster economic data from China, prospects the ECB will continue to lower interest rates in the coming meetings and fresh corporate results. On Thursday, the ECB cut rates by 25 basis points to 3.25% as expected.

Coming up in the session: A lighter day on charts.

In the bond market, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was up 2 basis points to 4.11%.

Germany's 10-year yield was up 1 basis point to 2.22%.

UK's 10-year yield was up 2 basis points to 4.11%.

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (EWG), (GF), (EWI), (EWQ), (FGM), (DAX), (FLGR), (FXB), (EWU), (FKU), (EWUS), (FLGB), (GREK)

data.title: Mitsubishi buys stake in top Philippine fintech platform
data.description:
Mitsubishi (OTCPK:MSBHF) will buy a 50% stake in Ayala Corp.’s (OTCPK:AYALY) AC Ventures Holdings for a minimum of P18.4 billion (~$319 million). AC Ventures owns ~13% of Globe Fintech Innovations, (Mynt). Mynt has two fintech companies: G-Xchange- the mobile wallet operator of GCash, and Fuse Lending, a tech-based micro-lender.

This acquisition effectively grants Mitsubishi (OTCPK:MSBHF) a 6.5% stake Mynt, the operator of Philippines' biggest fintech platform GCash.

The partnership aims to enhance Mynt's growth both domestically and internationally, particularly in areas like cloud-based payments and new credit solutions.

The deal was disclosed by Ayala (OTCPK:AYALY) on October 18, 2024, and is subject to regulatory approvals.

The collaboration is seen as a renewal of a long-standing partnership that dates back to 1974, which has previously included projects across various sectors such as renewable energy and auto dealerships.

“We believe Mitsubishi can add meaningful value to Mynt, which will allow Mynt to deliver significant value to its over 94 million registered users. It’s all about serving better the many Filipinos that depend on GCash and Fuse, and for making a wider variety of financial and other products available to as many Filipinos as possible,” said Cezar Consing, President and CEO of Ayala (OTCPK:AYALY).

data.title: Toppan Holdings acquires HID's Citizen Identity Solutions business
data.description:
• Toppan Holdings (OTCPK:TOPPY) to acquire HID's CID (Citizen Identity Solutions) business area, a division of HID Global Corporation focused on the government identity sector.
  • At the forefront of secure government identity solutions, HID's CID business comes with over 20 years of experience, serving more than 50 countries worldwide.
  • Source: Press Release
